# @moku-labs/worker — Full LLM Reference

> Server-side Cloudflare Workers app + deploy framework built on @moku-labs/core. Cloudflare
> primitives (Durable Objects, Queues, R2, D1, KV) as composable Moku plugins, designed to compose
> alongside Moku Web (@moku-labs/web).
>
> Package: @moku-labs/worker @ 0.1.0
> Built on: @moku-labs/core @ 0.1.4
> Depends on: @moku-labs/common @ 0.1.1 (supplies the log + env core plugins)
> Engines: node >=24.0.0, bun >=1.3.14. Package manager: bun (never npm/yarn/pnpm).

================================================================================
1. ENTRY POINTS
================================================================================

The package exposes exactly ONE entry point (from package.json `exports`):

  "."  → src/index.ts  — the only entry. RUNTIME ships in the deployed Worker bundle; the NODE-ONLY
                         deploy/CLI plugins (deployPlugin/cliPlugin, using node:child_process +
                         node:fs) are exported from here too but tree-shaken out ("sideEffects":
                         false) of any bundle that does not list them in createApp({ plugins }) (HC11).

The specs reference `@moku-labs/worker/worker` and `@moku-labs/worker/cli` subpaths. NEITHER EXISTS.
There is only `.` — use `@moku-labs/worker` for both runtime and the (tree-shaken) deploy/CLI plugins.

Exports from "@moku-labs/worker" (src/index.ts) — the only entry point:
  Values:  createApp, createPlugin, endpoint, defineDurableObject,
           bindingsPlugin, serverPlugin, kvPlugin, d1Plugin, queuesPlugin, storagePlugin,
           durableObjectsPlugin, stagePlugin, envPlugin, logPlugin,
           deployPlugin, cliPlugin  (node-only; tree-shaken out unless listed in createApp({ plugins }))
  Types:   WorkerConfig, WorkerEvents, WorkerEnv, StageApi, ExternalManifest, ResourceManifest,
           type namespaces: Server, D1, Queues, Storage, DurableObjects
           (also flat: Endpoint, EndpointHandler, RequestContext from the server barrel)

(envPlugin / logPlugin are authored in @moku-labs/common and re-exported here for completeness.)

================================================================================
2. THREE-LAYER MOKU MODEL
================================================================================

Layer 1 — src/config.ts  (createCoreConfig)
  Declares the global Config + Events types and registers the core plugins. Verbatim:

    export type WorkerEnv = Record<string, unknown>;

    export type WorkerConfig = {
      stage: "production" | "development" | "test";
      name: string;
      compatibilityDate: string;
    };

    export type WorkerEvents = {
      "request:start": { method: string; path: string; requestId: string };
      "request:end": { method: string; path: string; status: number; ms: number };
      "deploy:phase": { phase: string; detail?: string };
      "deploy:complete": { url: string };
      "provision:resource": { kind: "kv" | "r2" | "d1" | "queue" | "do"; name: string };
    };

    const defaultConfig: WorkerConfig = {
      stage: "production",
      name: "moku-worker",
      compatibilityDate: ""
    };

    export const coreConfig = createCoreConfig<
      WorkerConfig, WorkerEvents, [typeof logPlugin, typeof envPlugin, typeof stagePlugin]
    >("moku-worker", { config: defaultConfig, plugins: [logPlugin, envPlugin, stagePlugin] });

    export const { createPlugin, createCore } = coreConfig;

Layer 2 — src/index.ts  (createCore)
  Builds the framework and wires `bindings` + `server` as defaults, then exposes the Layer-3 API:

    const framework = createCore(coreConfig, { plugins: [bindingsPlugin, serverPlugin] });
    export const { createApp, createPlugin } = framework;

  NOTE: the two blocks above are the FRAMEWORK's OWN config.ts / index.ts (framework-author
  internals). The resulting exported `createApp` therefore already carries the defaults
  [logPlugin, envPlugin, stagePlugin, bindingsPlugin, serverPlugin]. Consumers (Layer 3) do NOT
  repeat these — see §8 for consumer createApp usage.

Layer 3 — consumer app  (createApp)
  createApp({ config?, plugins?, pluginConfigs? }) → frozen app. Synchronous, built once per isolate
  at module load. Stateless Workers never call app.start()/app.stop(). `plugins` lists ONLY
  consumer-added plugins (resource plugins, deploy/cli, custom plugins) — never the five defaults;
  a duplicate plugin name throws `TypeError: [moku-worker] Duplicate plugin name: "X"`.

================================================================================
3. CONFIG SCHEMA (every field, type, default)
================================================================================

WorkerConfig (createApp({ config })) — flat, complete defaults:
  stage              "production" | "development" | "test"   default "production"
                       Forwarded into the stage plugin (pluginConfigs.stage.stage).
  name               string                                   default "moku-worker"
                       Used by deploy as wrangler `name` (ctx.global.name).
  compatibilityDate  string                                   default ""
                       Used by deploy as wrangler `compatibility_date`.

pluginConfigs.<name> — each flat with complete defaults; frozen after createApp:

  bindings:
    required           string[]                 default []     Binding names downstream plugins assert.

  server:
    endpoints          Endpoint[]               default []     Compiled into the matcher table.

  kv:
    binding            string                   default "KV"   CF KV binding name.

  d1:
    binding            string                   default "DB"   D1 binding name.
    migrations         string                   default ""     Migrations dir (deploy metadata only).

  queues:
    producers          string[]                 default []     Queue names produced to (deploy metadata).
    onMessage          (message: Message, env: WorkerEnv) => Promise<void>   default async () => {}
                         Declarative consumer handler; awaited per message in consume(). Return = ack,
                         throw = retry.

  storage:
    bucket             string                   default "ASSETS"   R2 bucket binding name.
    upload             string                   default ""         Dir uploaded at deploy time (deploy
                                                                   metadata only).

  durableObjects:
    bindings           Record<string, string>   default {}     logical name → CF DO binding name.

  stage:
    stage              "production"|"development"|"test"   default "production"   (fed from WorkerConfig.stage)

  deploy:  (node-only)
    configFile         string                   default "wrangler.jsonc"   Wrangler config file.
    ci                 boolean                  default false              CI / non-interactive mode.

  cli:  (node-only)
    port               number                   default 8787   Default local dev port.

================================================================================
4. FRAMEWORK API + HELPERS
================================================================================

createApp(opts): App
  opts = { config?: Partial<WorkerConfig>; plugins?: Plugin[]; pluginConfigs?: { <name>: <config> } }
  Returns a frozen app whose regular plugins are mounted on app.<name>. The final plugin list is
  [...framework defaults, ...your plugins] = [log, env, stage, bindings, server, ...opts.plugins], so
  `plugins` should list ONLY consumer-added plugins. Throws a TypeError when a declared `depends`
  target is missing from the resolved list or appears after the dependent, AND throws
  `TypeError: [moku-worker] Duplicate plugin name: "X"` if you re-list a default (e.g. bindings/server).

createPlugin(name, def): Plugin
  Authors a regular plugin. Generics are INFERRED from the definition — never pass explicit type
  arguments to createPlugin. See §9 for the extension pattern.

endpoint(path: string): EndpointBuilder    — pure, no ctx, safe before createApp.
  Builder verbs each produce a typed Endpoint:
    .get(h) → GET     .post(h) → POST   .put(h) → PUT     .patch(h) → PATCH
    .delete(h) → DELETE   .head(h) → HEAD   .options(h) → OPTIONS   .all(h) → ALL (any verb / cron)
  h: EndpointHandler = (ctx: RequestContext) => Response | Promise<Response>
  Path params: {name} required, {name?} optional; both surface on params as
  Record<string, string | undefined>; an absent optional param resolves to undefined.

defineDurableObject(name: string): DurableObjectBaseConstructor & { readonly doName: string }
  Pure factory → a base class the consumer `extends` and exports from worker.ts. The base ctor has
  Cloudflare's signature (ctx: DurableObjectState, env: WorkerEnv), stored as readonly this.ctx /
  this.env; a static doName captures `name`. A Moku plugin cannot export a top-level class, so the
  consumer owns the exported DO class; the plugin manages config/bindings/stubs.

================================================================================
5. PLUGIN REFERENCE (Config / Events / State / API per plugin)
================================================================================

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
bindings  — Micro · @moku-labs/worker · depends: none (binding-family root)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Stateless resolver over a request-supplied Cloudflare env object. Regular plugin (so downstream
binding plugins can depends:[bindingsPlugin] and reach it via ctx.require). env is NEVER stored.

Config:  { required: string[] }                 default { required: [] }
State:   none
Events:  none

API (app.bindings):
  require<T>(env: WorkerEnv, name: string): T
    Resolves binding `name` off env, narrowed to T. Throws a [moku-worker] Error when the binding is
    null/undefined. Falsy-but-bound values ("", 0, false) return as-is and never throw.
  has(env: WorkerEnv, name: string): boolean
    true when `name` resolves to a non-nullish value. Never throws.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
server  — Standard · @moku-labs/worker · depends: [bindingsPlugin]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Owns the request lifecycle: compiles Endpoints into a specificity-sorted matcher, dispatches fetch
and scheduled (cron) through it. Allocates a fresh RequestContext per call on the stack carrying env.
env is never stored. Response flows through the api RETURN VALUE, never through emit. Compilation
happens once at onInit.

Config:  { endpoints: Endpoint[] }              default { endpoints: [] }
State:   the compiled matcher table (mutable surface; built from a copy of config.endpoints)
Events (verbatim types):
  Server.ServerEvents = { "server:matched": { path: string; method: string } }   // plugin-local
  also emits global request:start / request:end (WorkerEvents)

API (app.server):
  handle(request: Request, env: WorkerEnv, exec: ExecutionContext): Promise<Response>
    Matches method + pathname; runs the matched EndpointHandler; returns its Response, or
    new Response("Not Found", { status: 404 }) on no match. Emits request:start → server:matched
    → request:end (observability; server:matched skipped on 404).
  scheduled(controller: ScheduledController, env: WorkerEnv, exec: ExecutionContext): Promise<void>
    Cron entry. Matches controller.cron against the table using method "ALL", builds a RequestContext
    (request/url synthesized as https://cron/<cron>), and AWAITS the matched handler. Resolves
    immediately if no endpoint matches. The Worker default export must await it.

Helper: endpoint(path) — see §4. Also reachable as serverPlugin.endpoint(...) (on serverPlugin.helpers).

RequestContext (threaded to every handler):
  request   Request                              incoming request (synthesized for cron)
  env       WorkerEnv                            per-request bindings — on the stack, never stored
  exec      ExecutionContext                     waitUntil / passThroughOnException
  params    Record<string, string | undefined>  path params from the matched endpoint
  url       URL                                  parsed request URL
  require   RequireFn                            cross-plugin reach: require(plugin) → that plugin's api
  has       (name: string) => boolean            presence check for an optional plugin by name

Matching: (1) specificity — literal seg = 2, required {name} = 1, optional {name?} = 0, summed;
(2) method-specific beats ALL on same path; (3) required outscores optional; (4) optional lets the
trailing segment be absent. Trailing slashes don't change matching; method compare is exact/case-sensitive.

Type namespace: Server.RequestContext, Server.Endpoint, Server.EndpointHandler, Server.ServerConfig,
Server.ServerEvents. Endpoint/EndpointHandler/RequestContext also re-exported flat from the barrel.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
kv  — Micro · @moku-labs/worker · depends: [bindingsPlugin]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Thin env-first wrapper over one KV namespace, resolved per request via bindings. No state, no events,
no lifecycle.

Config:  { binding: string }                    default { binding: "KV" }
State:   none
Events:  none

API (app.kv) — env-first; namespace resolved fresh each call via
  ctx.require(bindingsPlugin).require<KVNamespace>(env, ctx.config.binding):
  get(env, key): Promise<string | null>                          KVNamespace.get; null if absent.
  put(env, key, value, opts?: KVNamespacePutOptions): Promise<void>   expiration/expirationTtl/metadata.
  delete(env, key): Promise<void>                                no-op if absent.
  list(env, opts?: KVNamespaceListOptions): Promise<KVNamespaceListResult<unknown, string>>
  deployManifest(): { kind: "kv"; binding: string }              build-time, takes NO env.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
d1  — Standard · @moku-labs/worker · depends: [bindingsPlugin]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Thin typed wrappers over D1's prepare().bind(). Not an ORM. No state, no events, no lifecycle.
D1Database / D1PreparedStatement / D1Result are ambient @cloudflare/workers-types globals.

Config (D1.Config):  { binding: string; migrations: string }    defaults { binding: "DB", migrations: "" }
State:   none
Events:  none. Ctx typed PluginCtx<Config, Record<string, never>, WorkerEvents>.

API (app.d1) — env-first:
  query<T>(env, sql, ...params): Promise<D1Result<T>>     all<T>() — .results is T[].
  first<T>(env, sql, ...params): Promise<T | null>        first row or null.
  run(env, sql, ...params): Promise<D1Result>             write/DDL; .meta (rows_written, last_row_id).
  batch(env, stmts: D1PreparedStatement[]): Promise<D1Result[]>   atomic, one round-trip; order preserved.
                                                          Build stmts via prepare(env). NOT raw SQL.
  prepare(env): D1Database                                resolve the D1Database to build statements.
  deployManifest(): { kind: "d1"; binding: string; migrations: string }   build-time, NO env.

Type namespace: D1.Config, D1.DeployManifest, D1.Api, D1.D1Ctx.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
queues  — Standard · @moku-labs/worker · depends: [bindingsPlugin]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Producer + consumer for Cloudflare Queues. env never stored. Consumer is declarative config
(config.onMessage), not a post-createApp mutator. Message/MessageBatch/Queue/ExecutionContext are
ambient @cloudflare/workers-types globals.

Config (Queues.Config):
  { producers: string[]; onMessage: (message: Message, env: WorkerEnv) => Promise<void> }
  defaults: producers [], onMessage async () => {} (no-op)
State:   none
Events (verbatim):  Queues.QueueEvents = { "queue:message": { queue: string; messageId: string } }

API (app.queues):
  send(env, queue: string, body: unknown): Promise<void>          Queue.send; throws [moku-worker] if
                                                                   queue binding missing.
  sendBatch(env, queue: string, bodies: unknown[]): Promise<void> maps each body → { body }; Queue.sendBatch.
  consume(batch: MessageBatch, env, exec: ExecutionContext): Promise<void>
    Iterates batch.messages: AWAITS config.onMessage(message, env) (return=ack, throw=retry), then
    fire-and-forget emits queue:message { queue: batch.queue, messageId: m.id }. The Worker's queue()
    export must await it. `exec` is accepted to match CF's signature; currently reserved/unused.
  deployManifest(): { kind: "queue"; producers: string[] }        build-time; reads ctx.config.producers.

Type namespace: Queues.Config, Queues.Api, Queues.DeployManifest, Queues.QueueEvents, Queues.Ctx.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
storage  — Complex · @moku-labs/worker · depends: [bindingsPlugin]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
R2 object storage behind a provider-adapter seam (StorageProvider). Two impls behind one interface:
the real R2-backed provider (providers/r2.ts) and an in-memory test double (providers/memory.ts). The
public API is identical regardless. No state, no events, no lifecycle. R2 types are ambient globals.

Config (StorageConfig):  { upload: string; bucket: string }       defaults { upload: "", bucket: "ASSETS" }
State:   none
Events:  none

API (app.storage) — env-first; bucket resolved per call via the provider:
  get(env, key): Promise<R2ObjectBody | null>     .body is a ReadableStream; null if absent.
  put(env, key, value): Promise<R2Object>          value: ReadableStream | ArrayBuffer | ArrayBufferView
                                                   | string | Blob | null.
  delete(env, key): Promise<void>                  key may be string or string[]; absent keys no-op.
  list(env, opts?: R2ListOptions): Promise<R2Objects>   prefix/limit/cursor/delimiter; .objects/.truncated/.cursor.
  deployManifest(): { readonly kind: "r2"; readonly bucket: string; readonly upload: string }   build-time, NO env.

Types (Storage namespace): StorageApi, StorageConfig, StorageManifest, StorageProvider.
NOTE the seam is KEY-first (provider.get(key)) while the public API is ENV-first (api.get(env, key)).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
durableObjects  — Standard · @moku-labs/worker · depends: [bindingsPlugin]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Resolves DO stubs off env + ships defineDurableObject. env never stored. DO types are ambient globals.

Config (DurableObjects.Config):  { bindings: Record<string, string> }   default { bindings: {} }
  Maps a logical name (code, e.g. "counter") → CF DO binding name (wrangler/env, e.g. "COUNTER").
  An unmapped logical name falls back to itself.
State:   none
Events:  none. (A DO alarm() runs in its own isolate with no Moku ctx/emit; DO observability is the
         consumer's concern via external telemetry, not the Moku bus.)

API (app.durableObjects):
  get(env, logicalName: string, idName: string): DurableObjectStub
    SYNCHRONOUS (not a Promise). Resolves the namespace via
    ctx.require(bindingsPlugin).require<DurableObjectNamespace>(env, binding), derives a stable id
    with namespace.idFromName(idName), returns the addressed stub. Throws [moku-worker] if unbound.
  deployManifest(): { kind: "do"; bindings: Record<string, string> }   build-time, NO env.

Helper: defineDurableObject(name) — see §4. On durableObjectsPlugin.helpers + top-level re-export.
Type namespace: DurableObjects.Config, DurableObjects.DeployManifest, DurableObjects.Api, DurableObjects.Ctx.
Structural helper types DurableObjectBase / DurableObjectBaseConstructor live in helpers.ts; you
rarely name them — `extends defineDurableObject("Name")` infers everything.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
stage  — Nano · @moku-labs/worker · CORE plugin (flat-injected as ctx.stage)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Deployment-stage / dev-mode detection. No state, no events, no deps, no lifecycle. (Worker-local
because common's `env` is env-VARIABLE access, not stage detection.)

Config:  { stage: "production" | "development" | "test" }   default "production"
         (the framework forwards WorkerConfig.stage → pluginConfigs.stage.stage)
Events:  none

API (StageApi, ctx.stage):
  isDev(): boolean         true iff stage === "development".
  isProduction(): boolean  true iff stage === "production". (FALSE in "test".)
  current(): "production" | "development" | "test"   the raw stage as the literal union.
  NOTE: isDev() and isProduction() are BOTH false in "test" — not boolean inverses.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
deploy  — Complex · @moku-labs/worker (node-only, tree-shaken) · depends: [storage, kv, d1, queues, durableObjects]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Build-time deploy orchestrator. Pipeline: detect → provision → wrangler-config → upload → deploy.
Uses node:child_process (spawn wrangler) + node:fs (read/write wrangler config, walk upload dir).
Tree-shaken out of bundles that don't list it via "sideEffects": false (HC11). No state, no hooks, no
lifecycle. Reads each resource plugin's deployManifest() via ctx.require, gated by ctx.has(name).

Config:  { configFile: string; ci: boolean }    defaults { configFile: "wrangler.jsonc", ci: false }
         (CI credentials read from Node env CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN / CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID, never config)
Events:  none of its own; emits the global deploy:phase, provision:resource, deploy:complete.

API (app.deploy):
  run(opts?: { guided?: boolean; yes?: boolean; manifest?: ExternalManifest }): Promise<void>
    Full pipeline; emits, in order:
      deploy:phase {detect} → deploy:phase {provision} → (per resource) provision:resource {kind, name}
      → deploy:phase {wrangler-config} → deploy:phase {upload, detail:"<n> files"} (only if R2 upload dir)
      → deploy:phase {deploy} → deploy:complete {url}.
    Manifest source: omit opts.manifest → assembled from each PRESENT resource plugin's
    deployManifest() (gated by ctx.has); supply opts.manifest → used verbatim (universal/non-moku path).
    Throws the [moku-worker] wrangler subprocess Error on failure.
  dev(opts?: { port?: number; stage?: string; webBuild?: WebBuild; onChange?: OnChange }): Promise<void>
                                                   Cold-builds the site (webBuild), spawns wrangler dev
                                                   --port <port> --config <configFile> (default 8787), then
                                                   watches + rebuilds on change. webBuild = () => Promise<unknown>
                                                   (full cold build, e.g. () => web.cli.build()). onChange =
                                                   (changes: readonly string[]) => Promise<unknown> — the
                                                   INCREMENTAL per-change rebuild (e.g. c => web.cli.update(c));
                                                   when wired, each debounced change rebuilds only the changed
                                                   paths instead of a full webBuild().
  init(opts?: { ci?: boolean }): Promise<void>     Scaffolds a starter wrangler config at configFile
                                                   (and CI files when ci). IDEMPOTENT — an existing
                                                   config file is left untouched.

Provisioning + wrangler config per resource:
  storage {r2}     r2 bucket create <bucket>; if upload: r2 object put per file       → r2_buckets
  kv {kv}          kv namespace create <binding>                                       → kv_namespaces
  d1 {d1}          d1 create <binding>; if migrations: d1 migrations apply --local     → d1_databases
  queues {queue}   queues create <producer> per producer                              → queues.producers
  durableObjects {do}  none — config-only (no wrangler do create exists)              → durable_objects
  (R2/D1 names lower-case the binding; queue bindings upper-case the producer; id/database_id written "".)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cli  — Standard · @moku-labs/worker (node-only, tree-shaken) · depends: [deployPlugin]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Developer-facing dev/deploy verbs + a live progress TUI. Thin passthroughs to deploy; does no work
itself. No state, no events of its own, no lifecycle. The "TUI" is plain ctx.log lines (no argv
parser, no bin, no TUI library) — the consumer's script owns argument handling.

Config:  { port: number }                       default { port: 8787 }
Events:  none of its own. Hooks the three global deploy events for the TUI:
  deploy:phase        → "> <phase>"  (or "> <phase> - <detail>")
  provision:resource  → "  + <kind> <name>"
  deploy:complete     → "done -> <url>"

API (app.cli):
  dev(opts?: { port?: number; stage?: string; webBuild?: WebBuild; onChange?: OnChange }): Promise<void>
    → ctx.require(deployPlugin).dev(opts)   (forwards port/stage/webBuild/onChange)
      onChange = incremental per-change rebuild hook, e.g. changes => web.cli.update(changes); when omitted
      each change runs a full webBuild() instead (the prior behavior — backward compatible).
  deploy(opts?: { guided?: boolean; yes?: boolean }): Promise<void>
    → ctx.require(deployPlugin).run(opts)   (forwards opts verbatim; no opts → passes undefined)
    Surfaces only { guided?, yes? }; for the universal/manifest path call app.deploy.run({ manifest })
    directly.

================================================================================
6. COMPLETE EVENT CATALOG
================================================================================

GLOBAL events — type WorkerEvents (src/config.ts). Visible to every plugin; hookable without depends.
  request:start        { method: string; path: string; requestId: string }
                         emitted by server at the start of handle(), before matching. requestId is a
                         fresh crypto.randomUUID().
  request:end          { method: string; path: string; status: number; ms: number }
                         emitted by server after the handler returns (final status + elapsed ms).
  deploy:phase         { phase: string; detail?: string }
                         emitted by deploy at each stage: "detect", "provision", "wrangler-config",
                         "upload" (detail "<n> files"), "deploy".
  provision:resource   { kind: "kv" | "r2" | "d1" | "queue" | "do"; name: string }
                         emitted by deploy once per provisioned resource.
  deploy:complete      { url: string }
                         emitted by deploy after wrangler deploy succeeds.

PLUGIN-LOCAL events — declared on the producer; observers reach them via depends:[<plugin>].
  server:matched       { path: string; method: string }                 (Server.ServerEvents)
                         emitted by server after a request matches an endpoint, before the handler
                         runs. NOT emitted on 404.
  queue:message        { queue: string; messageId: string }             (Queues.QueueEvents)
                         emitted by queues after config.onMessage settles for a message inside consume.

Discipline: all emits are FIRE-AND-FORGET observability. Request/response and deploy WORK flow through
api return values, never through emit (the kernel cannot carry a value through an event). No plugin
emits another plugin's event via a cast.

================================================================================
7. DEPENDENCY GRAPH + INIT ORDER
================================================================================

  bindings (root — depends on nothing)
     ├── server
     ├── kv
     ├── d1
     ├── queues
     ├── storage
     └── durableObjects
  deploy → depends [storage, kv, d1, queues, durableObjects]      (node-only)
  cli    → depends [deploy]                                        (node-only)

Init order is a topological sort of this graph; bindings initializes first. Every `depends` target
MUST be present in the resolved plugin list AND registered earlier than the dependent, or createApp
throws a TypeError. bindings + server are FRAMEWORK DEFAULTS — the exported createApp already resolves
to [log, env, stage, bindings, server, ...your plugins], so a resource plugin's depends:[bindingsPlugin]
is already satisfied. Do NOT re-list bindingsPlugin/serverPlugin in createApp({ plugins }); a duplicate
name throws `TypeError: [moku-worker] Duplicate plugin name: "bindings"`. (Configure the defaults via
pluginConfigs.bindings / pluginConfigs.server.) Node deploy scripts still list the resource plugins +
deployPlugin/cliPlugin themselves — those are not defaults — but never bindings/server.

Why bindings is REGULAR (not core): core plugins cannot be require/depends targets, so the binding
family chains off a regular `bindings`. The CORE plugins (log, env, stage) are flat-injected on every
plugin's ctx (ctx.log, ctx.env, ctx.stage) instead of mounting on app.

================================================================================
8. END-TO-END USAGE EXAMPLES
================================================================================

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8.1  fetch handler using server + kv + d1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```typescript
// app.ts
import { createApp, d1Plugin, endpoint, kvPlugin } from "@moku-labs/worker";

type Product = { id: number; name: string; active: number };

// bindings + server are framework defaults — list ONLY the resource plugins you add.
export const app = createApp({
  config: { name: "shop-api", stage: "production", compatibilityDate: "2026-06-17" },
  plugins: [kvPlugin, d1Plugin],
  pluginConfigs: {
    kv: { binding: "CACHE" },
    d1: { binding: "DB", migrations: "./migrations" },
    server: {
      endpoints: [
        endpoint("/health").get(() => new Response("ok", { status: 200 })),

        // KV-cached read; fall through to D1 on a miss.
        endpoint("/products/{id}").get(async ({ params, env, require }) => {
          const id = params.id ?? "";
          const cached = await require(kvPlugin).get(env, `product:${id}`);
          if (cached !== null) return new Response(cached, { headers: { "x-cache": "hit" } });

          const row = await require(d1Plugin).first<Product>(
            env, "SELECT * FROM products WHERE id = ?", id
          );
          if (!row) return new Response(null, { status: 404 });

          const body = JSON.stringify(row);
          await require(kvPlugin).put(env, `product:${id}`, body, { expirationTtl: 300 });
          return new Response(body, { headers: { "x-cache": "miss" } });
        }),

        // Write path: D1 insert + cache invalidation.
        endpoint("/products").post(async ({ request, env, require }) => {
          const { name } = (await request.json()) as { name: string };
          const res = await require(d1Plugin).run(
            env, "INSERT INTO products (name, active) VALUES (?, 1)", name
          );
          return Response.json({ id: res.meta.last_row_id }, { status: 201 });
        })
      ]
    }
  }
});
```
```typescript
// worker.ts — hand-assembled default export.
import { app } from "./app";
export default {
  fetch: (request: Request, env: Record<string, unknown>, ctx: ExecutionContext) =>
    app.server.handle(request, env, ctx)
} satisfies ExportedHandler;
```

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8.2  Queue producer + consumer
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```typescript
// app.ts
import { createApp, endpoint, queuesPlugin } from "@moku-labs/worker";
import type { Message } from "@cloudflare/workers-types";

type Order = { orderId: string; total: number };

// bindings + server are framework defaults; pluginConfigs.bindings still configures the default.
export const app = createApp({
  plugins: [queuesPlugin],
  pluginConfigs: {
    bindings: { required: ["orders"] },
    queues: {
      producers: ["orders"],
      // Awaited once per message inside consume(); throw → Cloudflare retries.
      onMessage: async (message: Message, env) => {
        const order = message.body as Order;
        await fulfill(order, env);
      }
    },
    server: {
      endpoints: [
        // Producer side: enqueue from a request.
        endpoint("/api/orders").post(async ({ request, env, require }) => {
          await require(queuesPlugin).send(env, "orders", await request.json());
          return new Response(null, { status: 202 });
        })
      ]
    }
  }
});

declare function fulfill(order: Order, env: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<void>;
```
```typescript
// worker.ts — fetch (producer) + queue (consumer) both hand-assembled.
import type { ExecutionContext, ExportedHandler, MessageBatch } from "@cloudflare/workers-types";
import { app } from "./app";

export default {
  fetch: (request: Request, env: Record<string, unknown>, ctx: ExecutionContext) =>
    app.server.handle(request, env, ctx),
  // MUST await so the isolate survives the batch.
  queue: (batch: MessageBatch, env: Record<string, unknown>, ctx: ExecutionContext) =>
    app.queues.consume(batch, env, ctx)
} satisfies ExportedHandler;
```

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8.3  Durable Object class + stub access
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```typescript
// counter.ts — the DO class is an ordinary EXPORTED class on the helper base.
import { defineDurableObject } from "@moku-labs/worker";

export class Counter extends defineDurableObject("Counter") {
  async fetch(): Promise<Response> {
    const n = ((await this.ctx.storage.get<number>("n")) ?? 0) + 1;
    await this.ctx.storage.put("n", n);
    return Response.json({ n });
  }
}
```
```typescript
// app.ts
import { createApp, durableObjectsPlugin, endpoint } from "@moku-labs/worker";

// bindings + server are framework defaults — only durableObjects is consumer-added.
export const app = createApp({
  plugins: [durableObjectsPlugin],
  pluginConfigs: {
    durableObjects: { bindings: { counter: "COUNTER" } },
    server: {
      endpoints: [
        endpoint("/count/{room}").get(({ params, env, require }) =>
          require(durableObjectsPlugin)
            .get(env, "counter", params.room ?? "")   // synchronous → returns the stub
            .fetch("https://do/increment")
        )
      ]
    }
  }
});
```
```typescript
// worker.ts — export the DO class ALONGSIDE the default export so CF can instantiate it.
import { app } from "./app";
export { Counter } from "./counter";
export default {
  fetch: (request: Request, env: Record<string, unknown>, ctx: ExecutionContext) =>
    app.server.handle(request, env, ctx)
} satisfies ExportedHandler;
```

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8.4  Node deploy script using @moku-labs/worker
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```typescript
// scripts/deploy.ts — node-only; run with `bun scripts/deploy.ts` (or `node scripts/deploy.ts`).
import { createApp } from "@moku-labs/worker";
import {
  d1Plugin, deployPlugin, durableObjectsPlugin, kvPlugin, queuesPlugin, storagePlugin
} from "@moku-labs/worker";

// bindings + server are framework defaults; add the resource plugins + deployPlugin (NOT defaults).
// deploy depends on all five resource plugins, so each must appear before deployPlugin here.
const app = createApp({
  config: { name: "my-worker", compatibilityDate: "2026-06-17", stage: "production" },
  plugins: [
    storagePlugin, kvPlugin, d1Plugin, queuesPlugin, durableObjectsPlugin, deployPlugin
  ],
  pluginConfigs: {
    storage: { bucket: "ASSETS", upload: "./public" },
    kv: { binding: "CACHE" },
    d1: { binding: "DB", migrations: "./migrations" },
    queues: { producers: ["orders"] },
    durableObjects: { bindings: { Counter: "COUNTER" } },
    deploy: { configFile: "wrangler.jsonc", ci: false }
  }
});

await app.deploy.init();                                      // idempotent starter config
await app.deploy.run({ guided: !process.argv.includes("--ci") });
```
```typescript
// scripts/cli.ts — the cli plugin sits above deploy; same plugin order + cliPlugin last.
import { createApp } from "@moku-labs/worker";
import {
  cliPlugin, d1Plugin, deployPlugin, durableObjectsPlugin, kvPlugin, queuesPlugin, storagePlugin
} from "@moku-labs/worker";

// bindings + server are framework defaults; cli depends on deploy, so cliPlugin comes last.
const app = createApp({
  plugins: [
    storagePlugin, kvPlugin, d1Plugin, queuesPlugin, durableObjectsPlugin,
    deployPlugin, cliPlugin
  ],
  pluginConfigs: { cli: { port: 8787 } }
});

const command = process.argv[2];
if (command === "dev") {
  const portArg = process.argv[3];
  await app.cli.dev(portArg ? { port: Number(portArg) } : undefined);
} else if (command === "deploy") {
  await app.cli.deploy(process.argv.includes("--ci") ? { yes: true } : { guided: true });
} else {
  throw new Error(`unknown command: ${String(command)} (expected "dev" or "deploy")`);
}
```
Universal / non-moku path — deploy arbitrary Worker code from a supplied manifest (no resource plugins):
```typescript
await app.deploy.run({
  manifest: {
    name: "legacy-worker",
    compatibilityDate: "2026-06-17",
    resources: [{ kind: "kv", binding: "CACHE" }]
  }
});
```

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8.5  Observing events from a plugin's hooks
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```typescript
hooks: (register) => {
  register("deploy:phase", ({ phase, detail }) =>
    console.log(`> ${phase}${detail ? ` - ${detail}` : ""}`)
  );
  register("provision:resource", ({ kind, name }) => console.log(`  + ${kind} ${name}`));
  register("deploy:complete", ({ url }) => console.log(`done -> ${url}`));
}
```

================================================================================
9. ERROR-HANDLING PATTERNS ([moku-worker]-prefixed errors)
================================================================================

All framework-thrown errors carry a `[moku-worker]` prefix. Known cases:

- Unbound binding (from bindings.require, propagated by every resource plugin):
    `[moku-worker] binding "MY_KV" is not bound.`
  Thrown when config.binding is null/undefined on the request env. Falsy-but-bound values
  ("", 0, false) are returned as-is and do NOT throw. Use bindings.has(env, name) or the handler's
  has("kv") to branch on optional bindings instead of catching.

- Duplicate path param at server compile time (onInit):
    a [moku-worker]-prefixed Error when an endpoint path contains duplicate {param} names.

- Deploy subprocess failure (node-only):
    `[moku-worker] wrangler exited with code <n>`  or  `[moku-worker] Failed to spawn wrangler`
  run() / dev() propagate these from the wrangler subprocess on a non-zero exit / spawn failure.

- Dependency wiring (createApp): a TypeError when a declared `depends` target is missing from the
  resolved plugin list or appears after the dependent. (Not [moku-worker]-prefixed — it is a
  kernel-level TypeError surfaced at createApp.)

- Duplicate plugin name (createApp): `TypeError: [moku-worker] Duplicate plugin name: "X"` when
  `opts.plugins` re-lists a name already present — most often a framework default (bindings/server)
  or the same plugin added twice. Fix: list ONLY consumer-added plugins; configure defaults via
  pluginConfigs.

Handler errors are NOT swallowed: a handler that throws propagates out of app.server.handle to the
caller (server "does not throw on its own"). Wrap handler bodies in try/catch if you want to map
thrown errors to a Response.

================================================================================
10. EXTENSION PATTERN — authoring a custom plugin with createPlugin
================================================================================

createPlugin infers all generics from the definition — do NOT pass explicit type arguments.
A binding-family plugin declares depends:[bindingsPlugin] and resolves its binding per call:

```typescript
import { bindingsPlugin, createPlugin } from "@moku-labs/worker";
import type { WorkerEnv } from "@moku-labs/worker";

export const cachePlugin = createPlugin("cache", {
  depends: [bindingsPlugin] as const,
  config: { binding: "CACHE" },           // flat, complete defaults
  api: (ctx) => {
    // resolve the namespace fresh on each call — never capture env or the handle
    const ns = (env: WorkerEnv) =>
      ctx.require(bindingsPlugin).require<KVNamespace>(env, ctx.config.binding);
    return {
      read: (env: WorkerEnv, key: string) => ns(env).get(key),
      write: (env: WorkerEnv, key: string, value: string) => ns(env).put(key, value)
    };
  }
});
```

Owning a PLUGIN-LOCAL event — declare it via the events register-callback BEFORE the api factory so
the event map is inferred into ctx (then ctx.emit("metric:hit", …) is type-checked):

```typescript
export const metricsPlugin = createPlugin("metrics", {
  events: (register) => register.map<{ "metric:hit": { name: string } }>({
    "metric:hit": "a metric was recorded"
  }),
  api: (ctx) => ({
    hit: (name: string) => ctx.emit("metric:hit", { name })   // fire-and-forget
  })
});
```

Observing events (global or another plugin's local) — via hooks; for a plugin-local event you also
declare depends:[<producer>]:

```typescript
import { createPlugin, queuesPlugin } from "@moku-labs/worker";

export const queueMetricsPlugin = createPlugin("queueMetrics", {
  depends: [queuesPlugin] as const,
  hooks: {
    "queue:message": ({ queue, messageId }) => { /* record a metric, log, etc. */ }
  }
});
```

Register the instance: add `export { cachePlugin } from "./cache";` to src/plugins/index.ts (runtime)
or src/cli.ts (node-only). Mount point: regular plugins appear on app.<name> (here app.cache).
Conventions: no explicit generics on createPlugin; omit onInit/onStart/onStop unless a plugin truly
has work to do at those phases (Workers are request-scoped — most plugins need none); thread env as
the first argument and never store it; emit only your OWN events; do request/response WORK through
api return values, not emit.

================================================================================
11. DESIGN INVARIANTS (quick recall)
================================================================================

- env-per-request: first argument to every binding method; on the stack only; never in state.
- Response/work via return value, never via emit (events are observability only).
- bindings is the regular-plugin root of the binding family (core plugins can't be depends targets).
- deploy/cli are node-only; exported from @moku-labs/worker, tree-shaken out of the Worker bundle (HC11).
- deploy reads deployManifest() apis, never sibling pluginConfigs (a plugin sees ctx.global + its own
  ctx.config; require returns api, not config).
- createApp is synchronous + frozen; stateless Workers never call start()/stop().
- The Worker default export ({ fetch, scheduled, queue }) is hand-assembled by the consumer; no plugin
  produces it.
- Cloudflare types (KVNamespace, D1Database, R2Bucket, Queue, DurableObject*, ExecutionContext, …) are
  ambient @cloudflare/workers-types globals — used unqualified, never imported, never bundled.
